coverage.tool: TUnit --coverage (crashed in WSL2 — using static analysis instead)
coverage.note: Coverage tool fails with PipeConnection exception. Analysis based on code review and test-to-code mapping.

findings.issue: Steps.RunAsync discards the return value of Setup.Record() at line 47. Code analyzer v1 flagged Record as 'silently swallowing errors'. Coder v2 changed Record's return type to Task<Data>, but the call site still ignores the result. If DataSource.Set fails (disk full, locked DB), the step won't be marked as executed and will silently re-run on next startup — but the current setup execution proceeds as if nothing happened. No test covers this path.
findings.impact: A recording failure is invisible to the caller. Depending on intent, this is either a graceful degradation (re-run is safe) or a silent data loss bug. Either way, the behavior should be documented and tested.
findings.suggestion: Option A: If discarding is intentional (re-run is safe), add a comment in Steps.RunAsync explaining the decision and add a test that verifies setup completes even when Record fails. Option B: If recording failure should abort setup, check the return value and propagate the error.

findings.issue: RunAsync_SkipsAlreadyExecutedSteps doesn't prove step1 was actually skipped. It pre-records step1, runs setup, then checks both steps are recorded. But re-executing step1 and re-recording it would produce the same result. The 'skip' behavior (line 36-37 of Steps.RunAsync) has no direct verification.
findings.impact: Low — the skip logic is simple (if IsExecuted, continue) and is implicitly tested. But the test name promises more than it delivers.
findings.suggestion: Use a spy/counter action to verify step1's action was NOT executed. Or check the DataSource timestamp — the pre-recorded entry shouldn't have a different timestamp after RunAsync.

findings.issue: No PLang .goal test for the Setup feature. The full pipeline (builder → .pr with IsSetup=true → GoalMapper → runtime run-once semantics) is untested. All 11 Setup tests are C# unit tests with manually constructed Goal/Step objects.
findings.impact: Builder could generate incorrect .pr files for Setup.goal syntax and no test would catch it. However, setup goals use the same step syntax as regular goals, so the builder risk is low.
findings.suggestion: Add a PLang test that creates a Setup.goal with a step, runs it twice, and verifies the step only executes once. This requires the test runner to support setup execution or a custom test harness.

findings.issue: Cancellation check at line 56-57 of Steps.RunAsync is untested. No test passes a cancelled token during step execution.
findings.impact: Low — cancellation is a standard pattern and unlikely to be broken.
findings.suggestion: Add a test that starts RunAsync with a pre-cancelled token and verifies it returns GoalError.Cancelled.

summary: All tests green: 1478 C# tests pass, 23/23 PLang tests pass. Setup system has 11 solid C# tests covering goal ordering, exclusion from lookup, IsExecuted/Record persistence, skip-already-executed, hash-change re-run, context propagation, failed-step-not-recorded, and tolerated-error-recorded. Code analyzer v4 approved. One notable gap: Steps.RunAsync discards Record()'s return value (code analyzer fix was incomplete — return type changed but caller ignores result). Minor: skip test is weak (doesn't prove skip happened), no PLang integration test, cancellation untested.
